content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Ms. Kristin Gaspar 
Encinitas City Mayor 
748 Lynwood Drive 
Encinitas, CA 92024 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-033 
Dear Ms. Gaspar: 
March 14, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Refonn Act (the "Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section I 090. 
QUESTION 
Under the Act, may you participate in decisions concerning a lawsuit filed against the City 
by a property development firm whose attorney is a client of your physical therapy company? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. You do not have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act prohibiting you from 
participating in matters concerning the lawsuit filed against the City by the property development 
firm DCM Properties ("DCM"). 
FACTS 
You are the Mayor for the City of Encinitas. DCM has served the City with a lawsuit. DCM 
has retained attorney, Greg Day, for legal representation in the lawsuit against the City. You and 
your husband own a physical therapy company (Gaspar Physical Therapy). Mr. Day has been a 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-033 
Page No. 2 
client of your physical therapy company for over three years. He has been in your office within the 
past six months for physical therapy and his bills have exceeded $500.2 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
Section 87103 defines interest from which a conflict of interest may arise and includes any source 
of income amounting to a total of at least $500, provided or promised to, and received by the public 
official within 12 months before the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
Based on the information you have provided, Mr. Day is a source of income to your 
physical therapy company. He has received physical therapy from your office within the past six 
months and his payments to your business have exceeded $500. Therefore, Mr. Day is a source of 
income to your business. Accordingly, you have a financial interest in Mr. Day as a source of 
income to you. 
Foreseeability: A conflict of interest arises when the reasonably foreseeable financial effect 
of a governmental decision on a public official's interest is material. The standard for foreseeability 
differs depending on whether or not an interest is explicitly involved in the governmental decision. 
Pursuant to Regulation 1870 I (a), an interest is directly involved in a governmental decision, 
and the financial effect of the decision is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable, if the interest is a 
named party in, or the subject of, the governmental decision. 
Under the facts presented, Mr. Day is not explicitly involved in the lawsuit filed against the 
City by his client DCM. Accordingly, "[a] financial effect need not be likely to be considered 
reasonably foreseeable. In general, if the financial effect can be recognized a realistic possibility 
and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably foreseeable." (Regulation 18701(b).) 
Moreover, your facts indicate that decisions concerning the lawsuit would not have a 
reasonably foreseeable financial effect on the claimant's attorney, Mr. Day. We assume Mr. Day 
will be financially compensated for representing DCM regardless of the governmental decisions 
made by the City concerning the lawsuit. 3 
Accordingly, we find that the governmental decisions concerning the lawsuit filed against 
the City by DCM would not have a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on Mr. Day, who is the 
legal representative of DCM, and who is a source of income to your physical therapy company. 
2 You also utilize Greg Day's legal services for legal matters concerning your physical therapy company. 
However, this does not affect the conflicts of interest analysis for purposes of your participation in matters surrounding 
the lawsuit filed against the City. 
J You have not indicated any potential financial impacts of the DCM lawsuit on Mr. Day. However, if Mr. Day 
will receive a measurable financial benefit or loss from the City's decisions related to the litigation (e.g. he is 
representing DCM on a contingency basis and his compensation is based on the outcome of the litigation), then you 
should seek further advice. 


File No. A-16-033 
Page No. 3 
Therefore, you do not have a conflict on interest in decisions concerning the lawsuit filed 
against the City by DCM. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel

\

However, this does not affect the conflicts of interest analysis for purposes of your participation in matters surrounding
